palette.name: FIFA 26 Black
palette.usage: Primary brand base; emblem and dark backgrounds (official core colour)

palette.name: FIFA 26 White
palette.usage: Negative space, text on dark, clean page background (official core colour)

palette.name: Trophy Gold
palette.usage: Accent / highlights; echoes the photoreal World Cup trophy in the '26' emblem (approx.)

palette.name: Trophy Gold Light
palette.usage: Soft gold for hover states, dividers, medal/champion accents (approx.)

palette.name: US Blue
palette.usage: USA host theme; headers, links, primary UI accent (approx., USA = three blue tones)

palette.name: US Blue Light
palette.usage: Secondary USA blue tone for charts, tags, gradients (approx.)

palette.name: Canada Red
palette.usage: Canada host theme; alerts, highlights, secondary accent (approx., Canada = three red tones)

palette.name: Canada Red Deep
palette.usage: Darker Canadian red tone for emphasis/borders (approx.)

palette.name: Mexico Green
palette.usage: Mexico host theme; success states, pitch-adjacent accents (approx., Mexico = three green tones; Mexican flag green)

palette.name: Mexico Green Bright
palette.usage: Brighter Mexican green tone for badges and data viz (approx.)

palette.name: Neutral Mist
palette.usage: Light neutral background to keep the black/white/gold core breathing room (approx.)

design_notes: Official brand = "WE ARE 26" (unveiled May 2023, Los Angeles). Emblem: photorealistic World Cup trophy layered over a stacked "26"; the numerals are built from 48 geometric modules (squares + quarter-circles = pitch + ball), representing the 48 teams. Core palette is deliberately neutral — black, white and trophy gold — designed so the 16 host-city sub-brands and three host-nation tone families can be layered on top: USA = three blue tones, Canada = three red tones, Mexico = three green tones (per FIFA unveiling coverage). Typography: custom display face "FWC 26" — bold, chunky, condensed, inspired by street-soccer graphics and vintage sports posters — with Noto Sans as the secondary/system font. Each host city has its own poster-style identity riffing on the emblem (e.g. LA beach waves, San Francisco Golden Gate, Kansas City jazz/BBQ motifs), giving a vibrant retro-poster collage feel over the monochrome core. IMPORTANT: FIFA has not published exact public hex values; all hex codes above are well-reasoned approximations from flag colours and brand imagery (gold approximated from trophy renders; US blue brightened from Old Glory blue; Canada red from maple-leaf print red; Mexico green from flag green). Recommended webpage use: black/white base + gold for hero/emblem moments, with the blue/red/green tricolour as functional accents.
venues: MetLife Stadium (FIFA: New York New Jersey Stadium) — East Rutherford, New Jersey, USA (final venue)
AT&T Stadium (FIFA: Dallas Stadium) — Arlington, Texas, USA
SoFi Stadium (FIFA: Los Angeles Stadium) — Inglewood, California, USA
Arrowhead Stadium (FIFA: Kansas City Stadium) — Kansas City, Missouri, USA
Levi's Stadium (FIFA: San Francisco Bay Area Stadium) — Santa Clara, California, USA
NRG Stadium (FIFA: Houston Stadium) — Houston, Texas, USA
Lincoln Financial Field (FIFA: Philadelphia Stadium) — Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA
Mercedes-Benz Stadium (FIFA: Atlanta Stadium) — Atlanta, Georgia, USA
Lumen Field (FIFA: Seattle Stadium) — Seattle, Washington, USA
Hard Rock Stadium (FIFA: Miami Stadium) — Miami Gardens, Florida, USA
Gillette Stadium (FIFA: Boston Stadium) — Foxborough, Massachusetts, USA
Estadio Azteca (FIFA: Estadio Ciudad de México / Mexico City Stadium) — Mexico City, Mexico (opening match)
Estadio BBVA (FIFA: Estadio Monterrey) — Monterrey, Mexico
Estadio Akron (FIFA: Estadio Guadalajara) — Guadalajara (Zapopan), Mexico
BC Place (FIFA: BC Place Vancouver) — Vancouver, Canada
BMO Field (FIFA: Toronto Stadium) — Toronto, Canada
sources: https://1000logos.net/news/2026-fifa-world-cup-identity-introduces-new-design-system/ (emblem 48 shapes, host-city logos, Noto Sans secondary font)
https://panamericanworld.com/en/magazine/sports/branding-2026-fifa-world-cup/ (black/white/gold core palette, FWC 2026 typeface, host-city poster identities)
https://fwcumc.com/news/world-cup-logo-design/ (logo meaning and colours; host-nation blue/red/green tone families)
Note: exact official hex values unverified/not publicly published; palette hexes are reasoned approximations
